message:
feat(wills-estate): wire hero illustration into article + close hero/OG beads

Import and render wills_estate_post_illustration.webp in the hero section
with blur placeholder, priority loading, and a purple/cyan radial glow —
matching the slack article's illustration pattern.

Closes hgjp.7 (hero section) and hgjp.26 (OG images, already complete).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -183,7 +183,7 @@
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","title":"IntakePhasesViz: 9-phase flow with drill-in","description":"Implement `IntakePhasesViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Horizontal flow on desktop; vertical stack on mobile.
- 9 nodes: Orientation → People → Assets & Liabilities → Beneficiary Audit → Family Dynamics → Goals & Values → Incapacity → Jurisdiction → Wealth-Tier Routing.
- Each node shows:
  - Phase number + name
  - Approximate time (5 / 10 / 15 min per SKILL.md)
  - An icon from lucide-react
- Connected by arrows. Active phase highlighted.

## Scroll-linked highlighting
The articles sample-session section walks through a narrative that enters each phase in order. Use IntersectionObserver on paragraph markers to highlight the current phase as the reader scrolls.

## Click-to-drill
Click any node → inline panel under the viz shows:
- The agents opening question for that phase (plain English)
- The typical artifact written in that phase (e.g., `intake-record.md` updated, `red-flag-triage.md` created)
- Branch points (e.g., "if user says blended family, trigger overlay")

## Interactions
- Keyboard: arrow keys to walk, Enter to drill.
- Touch: tap to drill; the scroll-link still highlights the phase the reader is "in."
- Reduced motion: no arrow animations; still highlights correctly.

## Acceptance criteria
- Scroll-link works smoothly and doesnt flicker.
- Drill-in copy is accurate to SKILL.md section "The 9 Intake Phases."
- Responsive at 320px through 1440px.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:41.162156518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:33:56.343393514Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:33:56.340569417Z","close_reason":"Code complete, verified by IvoryOrchid","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:41.162156518Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.491176707Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Scroll-linking implementation detail — use IntersectionObserver on [data-intake-phase='N'] markers placed by the sample-session section (hgjp.14). Fall back to click-driven active state if no markers are visible (e.g., if reader jumps from TOC to #produces, which has no intake-phase markers).","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.24","title":"DeliverablesTreeViz: 45-leaf interactive folder tree","description":"Implement `DeliverablesTreeViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Collapsible folder tree: top-level `my-estate-plan/` → three child folders `intake/`, `analyses/`, `deliverables/`.
- Leaf count: 2 + 20 + 23 = 45 files (exact match to the output contract in SKILL.md).
- Files represented as subtle pill rows with filename, icon, and tier-color (intake = gray, analyses = blue, deliverables = emerald).
- Mode-dependent files show a small "mode:" tag on hover (e.g., `business-continuity-activation.md` shows `mode: business-owner-succession`).

## Filters (toolbar above tree)
- **Show only**: All / Intake / Analyses / Deliverables.
- **Mode filter**: All / new-plan / existing-plan-audit / life-event-delta / urgent-bedside-signing / executor-activation / business-owner-succession / uhnw-restructure / maintenance-review.
- **Search input**: matches filename or plain-English purpose.

## Click-a-leaf behavior
Opens an inline detail card below the tree with:
- Filename
- 2-sentence plain-English purpose
- Representative snippet (2-5 lines) adapted from the template in `assets/` (lightly paraphrased for the web; we do not publish the full templates here — those are behind the paywall).
- Icon link to the relevant section of SKILL.md on the jeffreys-skills.md catalog page.

## Data source
A static const array at the top of the viz file, hand-curated from the bead Im reading now + `assets/` directory listing. Each entry: `{ path, category, purpose, modes[], sampleLines }`. Commit to writing all 45 entries by hand to keep them high-quality; do not scrape.

## Interactions
- Keyboard: tree is a proper `role="tree"` with up/down/left/right.
- Touch: tap to expand/collapse folders; tap leaves to open detail.
- Reduced motion: no expand/collapse animation, snap.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 45 leaves render.
- Filter combinations work (e.g., Mode=executor-activation shows fewer leaves highlighted).
- Detail card accurate.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:57.594122273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:42:54.651606478Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:42:54.648738348Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.24","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:57.594122273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.24","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.626927541Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","title":"AntiPatternCardsViz: 13-card flip grid","description":"Implement `AntiPatternCardsViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Responsive grid (4-col desktop / 2-col tablet / 1-col mobile) of flip-cards.
- ≥13 cards (one per anti-pattern listed in the anti-patterns section bead).
- Front of card: evocative pattern name + one-line hook + emoji/icon.
- Back of card: what actually goes wrong, worst-case outcome, which subagent the skill uses to catch it.

## Interactions
- Hover (desktop) or tap (mobile) to flip. Reduced motion: dissolve instead of 3D flip.
- Keyboard: Tab to focus, Space/Enter to flip.
- Cards are buttons with `aria-pressed` state for screen readers.
- On focus, a subtle ring appears matching the design system accent.

## Data source
Hand-authored array of `{ name, hook, explanation, worstCase, subagent }` objects. Use the 13-card starter list in the anti-patterns section bead.

## Style
Visual weight matches the slack articles callout cards. Each card has a subtle gradient border; hover glow.

## Acceptance criteria
- All cards flip.
- Reading the backs of the 13 cards gives the reader a comprehensive enough picture of the skills risk-scanning layer.
- Accessible: cards should be reachable and readable with keyboard alone.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:08.414151481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:43:19.224994800Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:43:19.214150858Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:08.414151481Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.768018958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","title":"OpenGraph + Twitter images (app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/opengraph-image.tsx, twitter-image.tsx)","description":"Create the two image-route files for the article, modeled on the slack articles equivalents.

## Files
- `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/opengraph-image.tsx`
- `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/twitter-image.tsx`

Use Nexts `ImageResponse` (next/og) to generate 1200×630 (OG) and 1200×600 (Twitter summary-large-image) cards at build time.

## Design
Match the visual identity of the slack articles OG card but rebrand for wills:
- Dark background (same `#020204` base).
- Title: "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan on a Saturday."
- Subtitle: "AI agents · paid skill · attorney signs the docs."
- Faint illustration echo (could reuse the parrot motif from the hero, or a cleaner document stack).
- Author badge at bottom-left: "Jeffrey Emanuel · jeffreyemanuel.com".

## Dependencies
- `@/assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp` (from hero-illustration bead).
- Fonts loaded via the `fetch` pattern used in the existing OG files.

## Acceptance criteria
- Both images generated at build time (`bun run build` produces them).
- URLs render correctly when shared on Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Slack, and iMessage.
- File sizes kept reasonable (<300 KB each).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:54:02.892028700Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.051860361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

- `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/twitter-image.tsx`

Use Nexts `ImageResponse` (next/og) to generate 1200×630 (OG) and 1200×600 (Twitter summary-large-image) cards at build time.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","title":"CSS styles: decide on sm- reuse vs wep- namespace (globals.css)","description":"Audit `app/globals.css` for every `sm-*` class the slack article uses (sm-scope, sm-body, sm-progress-bar, sm-drop-cap, sm-section-title, sm-subheading, sm-subheading-eyebrow, sm-code-block, sm-table-wrap, sm-table, sm-toc, sm-viz-wrap, sm-callout, sm-callout-emerald, sm-insight-card, sm-sign-off, sm-decisions, sm-display-title, sm-fade-section, sm-visible, sm-reveal-ready, etc.).

## Decision
Two options:
- **Reuse `sm-*`**: simplest. Zero new CSS. Works out of the box if the wills article uses the same visual language.
- **Namespace to `wep-*`**: safer if we expect the wills articles visual identity to diverge (e.g., warmer palette, softer shadows). Adds ~800 lines of CSS.

Default: reuse. Only introduce `wep-*` if a section bead requires a visually distinct style that cannot be expressed as a modifier class (e.g., `sm-insight-card sm-insight-card--wep-warm`).

## Action
- Read `components/slack-migration-article.tsx`: list every `sm-` class referenced.
- Run the article through a dry build; confirm no missing classes.
- If any warm-palette variants are needed (e.g., an amber-accented callout for the "if I die tomorrow" deliverable), add them as modifier classes on the existing `sm-` definitions.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article renders correctly with the existing stylesheet.
- No CSS duplication unless strictly required.
- Any new additions documented with a brief comment at the top of the new block.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:38.052564575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:41:14.190879525Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:41:14.186701102Z","close_reason":"Audited the wills article against the shared sm-* stylesheet surface. The article's current classes are already covered by app/globals.css, so the right decision is to reuse the existing sm-* namespace with no new wep-* block or duplicate CSS.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:38.052564575Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:13.879883415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","title":"Accessibility pass: focus, alt, heading hierarchy, reduced motion","description":"Full a11y audit over the article once all sections and visualizations are in.

## Checklist
- [ ] Every interactive element has a visible focus ring (Tailwind `focus-visible:ring-*`).
- [ ] Heading hierarchy is correct: one `h1` (hero), then `h2` per section, `h3` per subsection. No skipped levels.
- [ ] Every `<Image>` has meaningful `alt` text; decorative elements use `alt=""`.
- [ ] Every visualization has a descriptive `aria-label` and, where appropriate, a `<figure>` + `<figcaption>` wrapper.
- [ ] All visualizations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (no animations when set).
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works through every visualization (Tab order makes sense; arrow keys where natural).
- [ ] Color contrast ≥ 4.5:1 for body text, ≥ 3:1 for large text; ≥ 3:1 for UI state indicators (focus rings, active states).
- [ ] Skip link present (already in site layout, verify not broken).
- [ ] `aria-live` regions for the install sections "copied to clipboard" feedback (if we add that inline here).
- [ ] TOC anchors match section IDs exactly.

## Tools
- `bun lint` (eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y should catch the bulk).
- Manual keyboard walk through the whole article.
- Axe DevTools in the browser.

## Acceptance criteria
- Zero a11y warnings from eslint.
- Axe clean on the rendered page.
- Keyboard walk through the article without a mouse: reader can get to every meaningful interaction.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:49.495786737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.354501572Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:49.495786737Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.203071126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.354021373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","title":"Lighthouse / Core Web Vitals check","description":"Run Lighthouse against the article on a dev build + production build.

## Targets
- **LCP** ≤ 2.5s (hero image must use `priority` + blur placeholder; already the pattern).
- **CLS** ≤ 0.1 (visualizations must reserve layout space before mount; use explicit width/height or `aspect-ratio`).
- **INP** ≤ 200ms (scroll-linked effects must be passive; visualizations must not block the main thread on mount).
- **Performance** ≥ 90.
- **Accessibility** = 100.
- **Best Practices** ≥ 95.
- **SEO** = 100.

## If targets fail
- Visualizations too heavy? Check bundle splits: each dynamic import should land in its own chunk. Consider further code-splitting (e.g., antipattern cards flip state machine).
- LCP late? Verify the hero image is in the initial HTML (not SSR-skipped), has `priority`, and uses a proper blur placeholder.
- CLS high? The usual suspect is a visualization whose height isnt reserved; add `min-h-[Npx]` to the viz wrapper.

## Acceptance criteria
- Lighthouse targets met on production build (Vercel preview deploy).
- Any failure is resolved at root cause before merge.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:00.532041834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.798249640Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:00.532041834Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.797700271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
@@ -204,7 +204,7 @@
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","title":"Draft/preview mode for the article (pre-launch reviewer sharing)","description":"# Preview mode / share-token for pre-launch article

## Why
The legal / UPL review bead (hgjp.39), the content QA bead (hgjp.34), and the non-technical friend walkthrough (hgjp.34) all benefit from sharing a URL to the article BEFORE it ships. Also: Jeffrey might want one or two trusted reviewers (an attorney, a friend who read the tax article) to eyeball it before it hits Twitter.

Today, if we merge to main, Vercel publishes to production. There's no middle state.

## The design

### Option A: Vercel preview deployments (simplest)
Every PR or non-main branch auto-gets a preview URL from Vercel. Share that URL with reviewers. This already exists; the "new" thing is just using it deliberately.

Pro: zero code. Con: Vercel preview URLs are publicly accessible (no auth); anyone with the URL sees the article. For a launch article this is probably fine.

### Option B: Preview-token gate on the production site
Add a small middleware that checks ?preview=<token> query param OR a cookie. If the current article is within a "pre-publish" date window AND no valid token is present, serve a 404 (or a "launching soon" landing).

- Reserved tokens stored in a new small config in the repo (or in a database if jsm.md is doing this for all articles — but the personal-site is a static-first Next site, so a small table or env-backed list is fine).
- Tokens are opaque strings; minted on demand via a personal script.
- Token activates the article; without it the route is hidden.

### Recommended: Option A + small polish
Use Vercel preview deployments as the share mechanism. Add a lightweight `draft: true` frontmatter/flag to the article metadata; when draft, Next explicitly removes the article from /sitemap.xml, from the writing index (hgjp.5 registration), and from the command palette. When draft=false (publish day), the article surfaces everywhere.

This is the cleanest separation: draft state is a simple boolean in the article's metadata, reviewed in the content QA pass, flipped atomically at launch.

## Implementation

### Article metadata
```tsx
export const metadata = {
  title: "...",
  description: "...",
  alternates: { canonical: "/writing/wills-and-estate-planning" },
  robots: { index: false, follow: false }, // while draft
};

const DRAFT = true; // flip to false on launch day
```

### lib/content.ts
Skip draft articles when building the writing index:
```ts
if (!post.draft) writingList.push(post);
```

### sitemap.ts
Same: omit draft articles.

### command-palette.tsx
Same: omit draft articles from search index.

### Publishing
- Throughout development, draft=true; shareable via vercel-<branch>-<sha>.vercel.app URLs.
- Launch commit flips draft=false + robots: index, follow.

## Tests
- Playwright: in draft mode, /writing does not list the article; /sitemap.xml does not include it; Cmd-K "wills" returns no result. Yet direct visit to /writing/wills-and-estate-planning still renders (for authorized reviewers sharing the URL).
- Launch mode (draft=false): article appears in /writing, /sitemap.xml, command palette.

## Acceptance criteria
- draft flag implemented and wired to all three surfaces.
- Publishing is one commit: flip the flag.
- Search engines do not index draft articles (meta robots noindex).
- Tests cover both states.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:59.171182267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:58.884555586Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:58.881681355Z","close_reason":"Made draft-mode publication surfaces consistent for MDX-backed writing entries: writing index, sitemap, RSS, API search, and route metadata now honor draft visibility while direct preview URLs stay renderable.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:59.171182267Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Draft-mode safety belt.
(1) meta robots: while draft=true, the page emits <meta name='robots' content='noindex, nofollow'>. Verified via Playwright test that fetches the draft URL and asserts the header.
(2) Accidental index monitoring: add a simple once-daily cron that fetches the draft article URL, inspects the response for the noindex meta tag; if missing, page the operator. Catches the case where someone flips draft=false earlier than intended.
(3) The draft flag must be checked in THREE places per the bead description: sitemap.ts, lib/content.ts writing list, command-palette search index. Test all three — easy to miss one.
(4) Preview URL shareability: Vercel preview URLs are guessable enough that a reviewer who shares a screenshot can't inadvertently leak the URL via metadata. If that's a concern, add HTTP Basic auth to Vercel preview (Vercel Pro feature) or shrink the review window.
(5) Launch procedure: the commit that flips draft=false should ONLY flip that flag. Not bundled with other changes. Diff is one line; trivially reviewable.
(6) Post-launch: after the draft flag flips and the article is live, remove the robots-noindex cron check (it's no longer needed).","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","title":"Register article in lib/content.ts + command palette","description":"Two small registrations so the article surfaces in the sites navigation:

## 1. `lib/content.ts`
Add an entry to whichever array drives the writing listing (likely `writingPosts` / `writing` / similar; grep for where slack-mattermost-migration is registered). Include:
- slug: `wills-and-estate-planning`
- title: final article title
- description: ~140 char hook suitable for the writing index card
- date: publication date
- tags: `["ai", "skills", "estate-planning", "wills", "life-planning"]`
- href: `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`
- hero image reference

## 2. `components/command-palette.tsx` (Cmd+K search)
Add to the navigation/search array so `Cmd+K` → "wills" finds the article. Pattern: same as how the slack-mattermost article is registered.

## Why this bead is separate
These are tiny mechanical edits in existing registry files. Splitting from the article component bead avoids merge conflict with content sections and keeps the article discoverable the moment it goes live.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article shows up on `/writing`.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","title":"Write agent-readable primer markdown (public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md)","description":"Write `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md` — a single-file primer that a paying subscriber can download from the article and paste into any fresh Claude / Codex / ChatGPT session to boot the agent into the wills-estate-planning mindset.

## Purpose
This is the offline-friendly equivalent of the skill itself. The full skill is 201 files, ~24k lines, ~1 MB — too big to paste into a context window. The primer is a lossy but coherent summary the agent can use even without the full skill installed. Acts as:

1. **Proof-of-value tease** for visitors who are not yet paying subscribers (they can see the shape of the skill).
2. **Emergency fallback** for a paying subscriber whose agent setup isnt cooperating — they can paste this and still get useful output.
3. **The thing the "Download the operator primer as Markdown" button on the article points at** (see `PRIMER_HREF` in the article scaffold bead).

## Structure (draws from SKILL.md at ~/jeffreys_skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill/)
- `# Wills & Estate Planning — Operator Primer`
- TL;DR: what estate planning really is (coordination, not drafting), the 11 axioms condensed to the 5 most important ones, and the 2026 OBBBA $15M exemption reality.
- The kernel: axioms 0-11 in one-liner form with a sentence of why each matters.
- Wealth-tier routing (Tier 1-5 with one sentence each).
- The 9 intake phases as a numbered list.
- The 9-step workflow as a numbered list.
- The top 15 anti-patterns.
- The verification-first discipline (live-law checks).
- Output contract summary (intake/, analyses/, deliverables/).
- A short disclaimer: this primer is educational; an attorney signs the documents.
- Footer: "To get the full skill with all 17 subagents, 45 deliverable templates, and 135 reference files, subscribe at https://jeffreys-skills.md/ and install via the instructions at https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill"

## Format
- Plain Markdown.
- ~8-12 KB (smaller than slack primers 185 KB — this is deliberately a condensed version, not a complete operator guide).
- Semantic headings so an agent can index it.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists at `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md`.
- Articles download button successfully serves it as `application/octet-stream` with the configured filename.
- Content is self-contained: an agent with just this primer and no skill installed can still conduct a useful basic estate-planning intake.","notes":"test","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:49:43.785078713Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:49:43.781833618Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"test comment","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:56:38Z"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Primer size target corrected from 8-12 KB to 30-60 KB (hard cap 200 KB). The primer is the **travelling alternative to the full skill** — if a non-subscriber pastes it into a fresh Claude/Codex/ChatGPT session, they should be able to conduct a meaningful 9-phase intake, name all 12 axioms + 14 operators, and produce a minimum-viable deliverable set (asset-inventory, beneficiary-map, red-flag-triage, letter-of-instruction, attorney-engagement-brief). This is the single highest-leverage marketing asset on the whole article. Trim state-specific appendices (volatile anyway) before trimming axioms/operators.","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:56:46Z"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.9","title":"Intro section: drop-cap + narrative hook + TOC","description":"Intro narrative — the part that converts curiosity into "I want this."

## Opening beat (drop-cap paragraph)
Lead with the human stakes, not the tech. Something like:

> "Almost everyone you know is going to die, eventually, without a serious plan for what happens to their money, their house, their pets, their data, or their kids. Some of them will leave handwritten notes a grieving spouse cant legally execute. Some will leave a will from 2011 that still names an ex. Some will leave nothing, and a county court will decide for them. This is not because they didnt care. Its because estate planning is expensive, emotionally heavy, and the lawyers who do it well charge $500+ an hour for the parts that are basically just asking you good questions and writing down your answers."

## Middle beats (2-4 paragraphs)
- Reference the X-post hook: "Five readers told me they saved more than $1,000 on their taxes using my tax-preparation skill. One saved $20,000. That was a $20/month subscription." → then the same pattern for wills: a skill that captures the judgment Sullivan & Cromwell would charge you $20-40k for an initial consultation, available for the same $20/month.
- Explicit scope: this is for U.S. domiciled individuals, federal + state + local.
- Important positioning: **the skill does not replace your attorney**. It produces drafts, audits, and an attorney handoff packet so your attorney spends 3 hours instead of 15 on your case. Lean into that — it builds trust and reduces legal-liability perception.
- What "an estate plan" actually is: Axiom 1 — a will + beneficiary forms + titling + POAs + healthcare directives + digital inventory + communication plan, all telling one coherent story. Foreshadow the core-insight section.

## TOC (nav, matches slack pattern)
```
<nav className="sm-toc" aria-label="Table of contents">
  <h3>Contents</h3>
  <ol>
    <li><a href="#install">Install the skill (no terminal)</a></li>
    <li><a href="#who">Who this is for (and who it isnt)</a></li>
    <li><a href="#insight">Why a will alone is not the plan</a></li>
    <li><a href="#intake">The 9 intake phases</a></li>
    <li><a href="#produces">The 45 artifacts you walk away with</a></li>
    <li><a href="#workflow">The 9-step workflow, narrated</a></li>
    <li><a href="#anti">Anti-patterns: the parrot, the ex-spouse, the springing POA</a></li>
    <li><a href="#attorney">Handing off to a real attorney</a></li>
    <li><a href="#pattern">A pattern, not a product</a></li>
  </ol>
</nav>
```

## Tone discipline
- Warm, plain-spoken, confident.
- Use real people and real stakes ("your spouse, trying to find the 401(k) beneficiary form in a file cabinet theyve never opened").
- Technical terms get `<J>` tooltips; dont explain them inline unless the word "Axiom 1" is load-bearing.

## Acceptance criteria
- Drop-cap renders (reuses `sm-drop-cap` class).
- TOC links land on the correct anchor IDs (anchor IDs are the section beads responsibility; coordinate).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:36:38.124256184Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:23:59.221204765Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:23:59.218543656Z","close_reason":"Intro section fully implemented in wills-estate-article.tsx lines 406-728: drop-cap, narrative hook, TOC, scroll-spy, stat chips, all complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.9","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:36:38.124256184Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.9","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.476261086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.9","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Every content section that the TOC targets must emit a stable anchor ID that matches exactly. Freezing the contract now: #install, #who, #insight, #intake, #produces, #workflow, #anti, #attorney, #pattern. Each section bead (hgjp.11, .12, .13, .14, .15, .16, .17, .18, .19) must use exactly the corresponding ID on its top-level SectionHeader. Also: every narrative paragraph in the sample-session section (hgjp.14) must emit data-intake-phase='N' on its outer element so the IntakePhasesViz scroll-linked highlighter (hgjp.23) has a DOM hook to follow. Missing this coordination is the single most likely source of post-launch regression.","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:56:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hio","title":"FEATURE: Source Code Research for All Flywheel Tools","description":"# FEATURE: Source Code Research for All Flywheel Tools

## Purpose
Before writing content for the /tldr page, we need accurate, detailed information about each tool.
This requires systematic source code review of all flywheel tools to extract:

1. **Accurate functionality description** - What does the code actually do?
2. **Implementation details** - What language? What frameworks? Performance characteristics?
3. **Integration points** - How does it actually connect to other tools in the ecosystem?
4. **Key features** - What are the standout capabilities?
5. **Usage patterns** - How is it typically invoked/used?

## Why Source Code Review is Critical
- Marketing copy can be vague; code doesn't lie
- Ensures technical accuracy
- Discovers integrations that might not be documented
- Provides specific implementation highlights (e.g., "60fps TUI via Bubble Tea")
- Validates claimed features

## Tools to Research (12+ total)

### Primary Flywheel Tools:
| Tool | Path | Language |
|------|------|----------|
| NTM | /data/projects/ntm | Go |
| SLB | /data/projects/slb | Go |
| MCP Agent Mail | /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail | TypeScript |
| Beads Viewer | /data/projects/beads_viewer | Go |
| UBS | /data/projects/ultimate_bug_scanner | Python |
| CM (CASS Memory) | /data/projects/cass_memory_system | TypeScript |
| CASS | /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search | Rust |

### Supporting Tools:
| Tool | Path | Language |
|------|------|----------|
| DCG | /data/projects/destructive_command_guard | Go |
| GIIL | /data/projects/giil | Go |
| XF | /data/projects/xf | Go |
| Source to Prompt TUI | /data/projects/source_to_prompt_tui | TypeScript/Bun |
| Repo Updater | /data/projects/repo_updater | Bash |
| Flywheel Setup | /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup | Bash |

## Research Output Format
Each tool research task should produce a structured summary:
```
Tool: [Name]
GitHub: [URL]
Language: [Primary language]
Stars: [Count]

## What It Does
[2-3 sentences - accurate based on code review]

## Why It's Useful
[2-3 sentences - value proposition]

## Implementation Highlights
- [Key tech/framework]
- [Performance characteristic]
- [Architectural pattern]

## Synergies
- [Tool A]: [How they integrate - specific to code]
- [Tool B]: [How they integrate]
```

## Execution Strategy
- Use subagents (Task tool with Explore agent) for each tool
- Run multiple research tasks in parallel where possible
- Focus on README, main entry points, and integration code
- Look for imports/dependencies that show connections

## Dependencies
This feature blocks content writing - we need accurate research before writing copy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:13:49.464036179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:39:10.293753069Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:39:10.293685434Z","close_reason":"All source code research tasks completed. Content for all 14 tools fully written in
